summary: Capture the implemented BuilderGate terminal stability work as a dedicated SRS scope covering bounded runtime limits, input backpressure, output scheduling, snapshot storage budgets, runtime residency, and split WebSocket routing/fallback.

ambiguities.question: Should BuilderGate stability requirements be registered under a new target/scope or folded into the active AI TUI recovery target?
ambiguities.recommended_answer: Use new target 0.5.5-buildergate-stability and new scope BGSTAB.

implied_requirements: Default websocket transport mode remains unified.
Control/recovery WebSocket messages must not be blocked by input backpressure.
Output overflow must recover through authoritative server snapshot or repair flows instead of unbounded accumulation.
Hidden terminal runtime eviction must not delete persistent workspace or tab state.
Split WebSocket output failure must not drop queued terminal output or disturb split-shadow control replay state.

semantic_equivalences.spec_term: input backpressure and control lane protection
bounded queues, snapshot budgets, and runtime residency
